title.value: A survey on automatic generation of medical imaging reports based on deep learning
title.evidence: A survey on automatic generation of medical imaging reports based on deep learning

authors.evidence: Ting Pang1*, Peigao Li1 and Lijie Zhao1

year.evidence: Received: 19 February 2023 Accepted: 9 May 2023 Published online: 18 May 2023

abstract.value: Recent advances in deep learning have shown great potential for the automatic generation of medical imaging reports. Deep learning techniques, inspired by image captioning, have made significant progress in the field of diagnostic report generation. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent research efforts in deep learning-based medical imaging report generation and proposes future directions in this field.
abstract.evidence: Recent advances in deep learning have shown great potential for the automatic generation of medical imaging reports. Deep learning techniques, inspired by image captioning, have made significant progress in the field of diagnostic report generation. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of recent research efforts in deep learning-based medical imaging report generation and proposes future directions in this field.

doi.notes: DOI not provided in the paper.

venue.notes: Publication venue not mentioned in the paper.

main_topic.value: Automatic generation of medical imaging reports using deep learning
main_topic.evidence: The primary aim of this manuscript is to present a systematic review of studies on deep learning-based medical imaging reports generation.

value: Deep learning techniques in medical imaging
sub_topics.evidence: We summarize and analyze the data set, architecture, application, and evaluation of deep learning-based medical imaging report generation. Specially, we survey the deep learning architectures used in diagnostic report generation, including hierarchical RNN-based frameworks, attention-based frameworks, and reinforcement learning-based frameworks.

value.name: IU X-Ray
value.description: Chest X-ray images paired with their corresponding diagnostic reports.

value.name: ChestX-ray14
value.description: Contains 112,120 frontal-view X-ray images of 30,805 unique patients.

value.name: MIMIC-CXR
value.description: A large publicly available dataset of chest radiographs with free-text radiology reports.

value.name: PadChest
value.description: A labeled large-scale chest X-ray dataset obtained from 67,000 patients.

data_sets.evidence: The current public data sets have greatly contributed to the development of deep learning for medical imaging report generation. Some examples of these data sets include Indiana University Chest XRay (IU X-Ray), ChestX-ray14, CheXpert, MIMIC Chest X-ray (MIMIC-CXR), and PadChest.

value.name: Hierarchical RNN-based framework
value.description: Uses a hierarchical LSTM consisting of a sentence LSTM and a word LSTM for generating long reports.

value.name: Attention-based framework
value.description: Combines image and language models using attention mechanisms to enhance report generation.

value.name: Reinforcement Learning-based framework
value.description: Utilizes a cooperative multi-agent system to optimize report generation.

architectures.evidence: We focus on medical report generation based on deep learning. Essentially, the paradigm follows a typical encoder–decoder architecture. Recent works focus on generating long-form text instead of single sentences. Attention mechanisms that focus on salient parts have been widely used in image captioning.

evaluation_metrics.evidence: BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR, and CIDER are commonly used evaluation metrics for medical image report generation.

performance.value: The paper identifies that the use of deep learning can significantly improve the accuracy and efficiency of medical imaging report generation.
performance.evidence: Automatic generation of diagnostic reports from medical images can significantly reduce the workload of report writing.

value: Imbalanced datasets leading to biased report generation.
Lack of public databases with diverse imaging modalities.
challenges.evidence: One major issue is the imbalanced nature of available data sets. There is a lack of public databases that include a variety of image modalities, such as pathology, ultrasound, and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI).

value: Establish public databases with a variety of image modalities.
Develop private datasets for clinical practice.
Create specific metrics for evaluating medical imaging reports.
future_directions.evidence: To address these challenges, we need to establish public databases with a variety of image modalities, as well as develop private data sets to address the limitations of medical images and complex annotations.

value: Ting Pang: Wrote the main manuscript text.
Peigao Li: Wrote the main manuscript text.
Lijie Zhao: Prepared figures and tables.
contributor_roles.evidence: TP and PL wrote the main manuscript text and LZ prepared figures and tables.
